issues.title: global-error.tsx duplicated identically across all 7 apps
issues.description: All 7 apps contain byte-for-byte identical global-error.tsx components (GlobalErrorBoundary). This is multi-line JSX logic (~35 lines each) duplicated across every app. Any visual or copy change must be made in 7 places.
issues.fix: Extract to packages/app-components/src/GlobalErrorBoundary.tsx. Each app's global-error.tsx becomes a 1-line re-export: `export { GlobalErrorBoundary as default } from '@monorepo/app-components'`. Note: Next.js requires global-error.tsx to be a default export at app root, so each app still needs its own file, but the implementation should be shared.

issues.title: MSWProvider, mocks/browser.ts, and mocks/server.ts duplicated identically in 3 apps
issues.description: Three apps (admin, payments, store) contain identical MSWProvider.tsx files that wrap SharedMSWProvider with a local `import('@/mocks/browser').then(m => m.worker)` getter. Additionally mocks/browser.ts and mocks/server.ts are byte-for-byte identical in all three apps.
issues.fix: The MSWProvider pattern is already almost correct — the `getWorker` lazy import must reference each app's local @/mocks/browser, so the pattern cannot be fully eliminated. However, mocks/browser.ts (`setupWorker(...handlers)`) and mocks/server.ts (`setupServer(...handlers)`) are pure boilerplate with no app-specific logic and could be factored into a shared/providers factory in the `shared` package that accepts the handlers array, or at minimum document that this is intentional boilerplate.

issues.title: Time conversion constants (MS_PER_SECOND, SECONDS_PER_MINUTE, MINUTES_PER_HOUR) defined in multiple feature domains
issues.description: The same time conversion constants (MS_PER_SECOND = 1000, SECONDS_PER_MINUTE = 60, MINUTES_PER_HOUR = 60) are defined independently in three separate locations. The packages/auth/src/domain/tokens.ts also defines local SECONDS_PER_MINUTE and MINUTES_PER_HOUR. The shared package already has packages/shared/src/constants/time.ts but it only contains QUERY-related time constants, not the raw conversion factors.

issues.title: securityHeaders array duplicated in all 7 next.config.ts files
issues.description: The `securityHeaders` array (8 header entries including CSP, HSTS, X-Frame-Options, etc.) is copy-pasted identically in all 7 apps' next.config.ts. A CSP or header policy change requires editing 7 files. The apps do already share a createVitestAliases factory pattern at the root.

patterns.description: Many single-line re-export files exist (e.g. useSupabaseAuth.ts, QueryProvider.tsx, ThemeProvider.tsx, ApiAuthBootstrap.tsx, environment.ts, ErrorContext.tsx) in each app's shared/ folder. These are intentional per the monorepo architecture — each app has its own namespace for imports and the shared package is the single source of truth. These are NOT DRY violations.

Four apps (studio, auth, admin, store) have identical auth/callback/route.ts that delegate to `handleOAuthCallback(request)` from `api/supabase/callback`. This is a required Next.js routing file per app and is intentionally a thin delegation wrapper — not a DRY violation.

patterns.pattern: SELLER_PROFILES_QUERY_KEY constant in store and payments
patterns.description: The string 'seller-profiles' is defined as SELLER_PROFILES_QUERY_KEY in both apps/payments/src/features/checkout/domain/constants.ts and apps/store/src/features/cart/domain/constants.ts. These are in separate apps with separate query caches, so sharing the same key string is coincidental and the constants should remain in their respective domains.
